name: Anomaly Model
author: Exergy ∞ LLC

entities.name: anomaly
entities.brief: An anomaly or phenomenon that challenges a theory
entities.description:
Represents an anomaly, phenomenon, or observation that appears to violate or challenge
the assumptions or claims of a physics theory. Anomalies are observations that don't
fit the expected behavior predicted by a theory and may indicate limitations,
errors, or the need for theory extension or replacement. Each anomaly is linked to
the specific assumptions and claims it appears to violate, helping identify which
aspects of the theory are challenged by the observation.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name of the anomaly or phenomenon (e.g., 'Mercury's Perihelion Precession', 'Ultraviolet Catastrophe')

fields.name: description
fields.description: Detailed description of the anomaly, what was observed, and why it challenges the theory

fields.name: theory_id
fields.description: Reference to the theory that this anomaly challenges or violates

fields.name: violated_assumptions
fields.description: List of assumptions that this anomaly appears to violate or challenge (nested children)

fields.name: violated_claims
fields.description: Array of claim IDs that this anomaly appears to violate or challenge

fields.name: discovery_year
fields.description: Year when this anomaly was first observed or discovered (four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

fields.name: explanation_theory_id
fields.description: Reference to the theory that explains or resolves this anomaly (if applicable)

fields.name: explanation
fields.description: Description of how the anomaly was explained or resolved, including the explanation year and mechanism. Format: 'Year: [year]. [description]' or just '[description]' if year is in explanation_theory_id's theory year.

fields.name: papers
fields.description: Array of paper IDs related to this anomaly (discovery papers, explanation papers, etc.)

fields.name: people
fields.description: Array of person IDs associated with this anomaly (discoverers, explainers, etc.)

entities.name: violated_assumption
entities.brief: An assumption that is violated by an anomaly
entities.description:
Represents a specific assumption from a theory that is violated or challenged by an anomaly.
This entity links anomalies to the specific assumptions they contradict, allowing detailed
tracking of which theoretical premises are challenged by observed phenomena.

fields.name: violated_assumption_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this violated assumption record

fields.name: assumption_id
fields.description: Reference to the assumption from the theory that is being violated

fields.name: violation_description
fields.description: Description of how this specific assumption is violated by the anomaly

description: Anomaly and violated-assumption entities: observations that challenge theories, linked to assumptions and claims.
